title: solidago arguta subsp. harrisii
intro: Solidago arguta subsp. harrisii, also known as mountain goldenrod, is a subspecies of the Solidago arguta plant, which belongs to the asteraceae family of flowering plants. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of North America, known for its yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Solidago arguta subsp. harrisii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
